  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Maintenance Personnel",
  "description": "Viju Industries Nigeria Limited is a leading player in Nigeria&rsquo;s food and beverage sector, recognized for its dedication to quality, innovation, and consumer satisfaction. Since its establishment, the company has built a strong reputation by producing nutritious and affordable products that have become household staples across the country. Its flagship offerings include Viju Milk Drink, Viju BB Star, and a wide variety of flavored yoghurts, fruit milk drinks, and refreshing beverages. The company also produces Mr. V Premium Water and V-Cool Soft Drinks, ensuring a diverse portfolio that caters to different tastes and age groups. Each product is crafted to meet international stndards while remaining accessible to Nigerian families.

Guided by values of innovation, quality assurance, accessibility, and sustainability, VIJU Industries continues to expand its product lines and distribution network. Its commitment to excellence has made it a trusted brand not only in Nigeria but also across West Africa. More than a manufacturer, VIJU Industries is a symbol of nourishment and reliability, enriching lives with products that inspire joy, promote well-being, and reflect the company&rsquo;s vision of making nutrition enjoyable and affordable for all.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Personnel

Job Location:&nbsp;Kwali, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job&nbsp;Description


	Holds an electrician&rsquo;s license, capable of welding, or has many years of mechanical maintenance experience.
	Familiar with mechanical and electrical principles, with strong hands-on ability, basic management knowledge, and a hardworking, resilient spirit.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Salary
N100,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;vijutalent@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Maintenance Personnel Job at Viju Industries Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Viju Industries Nigeria Limited is a leading player in Nigeria’s food and beverage sector, recognized for its dedication to quality, innovation, and consumer satisfaction. Since its establishment, the company has built a strong reputation by producing nutritious and affordable products that have become household staples across the country. Its flagship offerings include Viju Milk Drink, Viju BB Star, and a wide variety of flavored yoghurts, fruit milk drinks, and refreshing beverages. The company also produces Mr. V Premium Water and V-Cool Soft Drinks, ensuring a diverse portfolio that caters to different tastes and age groups. Each product is crafted to meet international stndards while remaining accessible to Nigerian families.

Guided by values of innovation, quality assurance, accessibility, and sustainability, VIJU Industries continues to expand its product lines and distribution network. Its commitment to excellence has made it a trusted brand not only in Nigeria but also across West Africa. More than a manufacturer, VIJU Industries is a symbol of nourishment and reliability, enriching lives with products that inspire joy, promote well-being, and reflect the company’s vision of making nutrition enjoyable and affordable for all.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Personnel

Job Location: Kwali, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Holds an electrician’s license, capable of welding, or has many years of mechanical maintenance experience.
	Familiar with mechanical and electrical principles, with strong hands-on ability, basic management knowledge, and a hardworking, resilient spirit.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Salary
N100,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Store Officer",
  "description": "Johngate Industrial Company Limited human safety and comfort in driving is our most valuable priority,that is while we are the best suppliers of quality automobile products in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Store Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Alakija, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
You will be responsible for:


	Receiving, inspecting, and recording all incoming goods.
	Proper storage and arrangement of stock items.
	Issuing items to departments based on approved requisitions.
	Maintaining accurate stock cards, bin cards, and system records.
	Conducting routine stock counts and reconciling variances.
	Monitoring stock levels and notifying management of shortages.
	Preventing stock losses through effective control measures.
	Preparing daily/weekly stock and variance reports.
	Ensuring proper documentation and compliance with inventory policies.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc in Business Administration, Accounting, or related field.
	Proven experience as a Store or Inventory Officer.
	Proficiency in QuickBooks is mandatory.
	Strong knowledge of inventory management practices.
	Good numerical and record-keeping skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;johngate1234hr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;Priority will be given to applicants living very close to Alakija and its environs.",

Store Officer Job at Johngate Industrial Company Limited




        
    

        
        Johngate Industrial Company Limited human safety and comfort in driving is our most valuable priority,that is while we are the best suppliers of quality automobile products in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Store Officer

Job Location: Alakija, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
You will be responsible for:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Receiving, inspecting, and recording all incoming goods.
	Proper storage and arrangement of stock items.
	Issuing items to departments based on approved requisitions.
	Maintaining accurate stock cards, bin cards, and system records.
	Conducting routine stock counts and reconciling variances.
	Monitoring stock levels and notifying management of shortages.
	Preventing stock losses through effective control measures.
	Preparing daily/weekly stock and variance reports.
	Ensuring proper documentation and compliance with inventory policies.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc in Business Administration, Accounting, or related field.
	Proven experience as a Store or Inventory Officer.
	Proficiency in QuickBooks is mandatory.
	Strong knowledge of inventory management practices.
	Good numerical and record-keeping skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Priority will be given to applicants living very close to Alakija and its environs.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Representative",
  "description": "Fort Knox Outsourcing is poised to serve the growing need of businesses in Nigeria&nbsp;By helping them to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing.&nbsp;We act as a &ldquo;Third Party Service Provider&rdquo; that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Sales Representative Job at Fort Knox Outsourcing




        
    

        
        Fort Knox Outsourcing is poised to serve the growing need of businesses in Nigeria By helping them to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a “Third Party Service Provider” that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Job Location: Wuse Zone 5, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities

	Prospect and approach new customers

	Meet sales targets and KPIs
	Maintain strong customer relationships
	Provide basic sales reports and feedback.

	Sales experience is a plus

	Self-motivated and target-driven.

N80,000 / month.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Pediatrician",
  "description": "At Open Hospital Limited, your health is our priority. Your comfort is our commitment. Quality Healthcare You Can Trust. Compassion You Can Feel. A privately owned healthcare facility under the Open Health Foundation, providing accessible, patient centered medical services for individuals and families in Abuja, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pediatrician

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a qualified and compassionate Paediatrician to provide medical care to infants, children, and adolescents.
	The successful candidate will diagnose and treat illnesses, monitor growth and development, and offer preventive healthcare advice to ensure the well-being of young patients.


Key Responsibilities


	Examine and diagnose medical conditions in infants, children, and adolescents.
	Monitor growth, development, and overall health of paediatric patients.
	Prescribe and administer medications and vaccines as needed.
	Provide and supervise immunisation services and preventive care, including health education, nutrition advice, and immunizations.
	Manage acute and chronic childhood diseases.
	Collaborate with parents and caregivers to develop effective treatment plans.
	Maintain accurate medical records for all paediatric patients.
	Refer patients to paediatric facility when advanced care is required.
	Work closely with medical officers and other healthcare professionals to ensure high-quality care.


Eligibility


	Medical degree (MBBS or equivalent) with specialization in Paediatrics.
	Valid medical license and fellowship certificates.
	Strong clinical and diagnostic skills specific to paediatric care.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work with children and families.
	Ability to work effectively in a hospital or clinical setting.


Preferred Qualifications:


	2&ndash;5 years of experience in paediatric care.
	Knowledge of paediatric immunizations, growth monitoring, and common childhood illnesses.
	Experience in neonatal and adolescent care is an advantage.
",

Pediatrician Job at  Open Hospital Limited




        
    

        
        At Open Hospital Limited, your health is our priority. Your comfort is our commitment. Quality Healthcare You Can Trust. Compassion You Can Feel. A privately owned healthcare facility under the Open Health Foundation, providing accessible, patient centered medical services for individuals and families in Abuja, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pediatrician

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a qualified and compassionate Paediatrician to provide medical care to infants, children, and adolescents.
	The successful candidate will diagnose and treat illnesses, monitor growth and development, and offer preventive healthcare advice to ensure the well-being of young patients.


Key Responsibilities


	Examine and diagnose medical conditions in infants, children, and adolescents.
	Monitor growth, development, and overall health of paediatric patients.
	Prescribe and administer medications and vaccines as needed.
	Provide and supervise immunisation services and preventive care, including health education, nutrition advice, and immunizations.
	Manage acute and chronic childhood diseases.
	Collaborate with parents and caregivers to develop effective treatment plans.
	Maintain accurate medical records for all paediatric patients.
	Refer patients to paediatric facility when advanced care is required.
	Work closely with medical officers and other healthcare professionals to ensure high-quality care.


Eligibility


	Medical degree (MBBS or equivalent) with specialization in Paediatrics.
	Valid medical license and fellowship certificates.
	Strong clinical and diagnostic skills specific to paediatric care.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work with children and families.
	Ability to work effectively in a hospital or clinical setting.


Preferred Qualifications:


	2–5 years of experience in paediatric care.
	Knowledge of paediatric immunizations, growth monitoring, and common childhood illnesses.
	Experience in neonatal and adolescent care is an advantage.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Real Strikers Security Services Limited is&nbsp;a national Licensed (Grade A) private security company. We provide security services of all kind to individuals, local, multinational and corporate organization both private and public sectors of the economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We need a highly efficient and detail-oriented Accountant who will support the finance team in daily accounting functions, specifically payroll administration, financial reporting, reconciliations, and maintaining accurate financial records.
	The role requires strong attention to detail, good analytical skills, and the ability to meet strict deadlines while ensuring compliance with statutory and internal financial policies.


Responsibilities


	Payroll Management.
	Post and process journal entries to ensure all business transactions are recorded
	Maintain general ledger accounts and assist with month-end and year-end closing activities
	Prepare and process vendor invoices, payments, and staff reimbursements
	Track customer invoices and ensure timely collections
	Perform daily, weekly, and monthly bank reconciliations
	Reconcile balance sheet accounts and investigate discrepancies
	Assist in preparing financial statements and management reports
	Support budgeting and forecasting processes
	Ensure accounting records comply with internal policies and regulatory standards
	Maintain proper documentation and filing of financial records
	Prepare schedules and documentation for internal and external audits


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting (BSc or equivalent)
	2 &ndash; 4 years of relevant accounting experience
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, or in view) is an advantage.
	Good understanding of accounting principles (IFRS/GAAP)
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting software (e.g., Sage, QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle)
	Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
	High level of accuracy, attention to detail, and organizational skills
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	Experience with reconciliations and financial reporting
	Ability to meet deadlines and work with minimal supervision
	Knowledge of tax filings and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume&nbsp;to:&nbsp;recruitment@realstrikersltd.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at Real Strikers Security Services Limited




        
    

        
        Real Strikers Security Services Limited is a national Licensed (Grade A) private security company. We provide security services of all kind to individuals, local, multinational and corporate organization both private and public sectors of the economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We need a highly efficient and detail-oriented Accountant who will support the finance team in daily accounting functions, specifically payroll administration, financial reporting, reconciliations, and maintaining accurate financial records.
	The role requires strong attention to detail, good analytical skills, and the ability to meet strict deadlines while ensuring compliance with statutory and internal financial policies.


Responsibilities


	Payroll Management.
	Post and process journal entries to ensure all business transactions are recorded
	Maintain general ledger accounts and assist with month-end and year-end closing activities
	Prepare and process vendor invoices, payments, and staff reimbursements
	Track customer invoices and ensure timely collections
	Perform daily, weekly, and monthly bank reconciliations
	Reconcile balance sheet accounts and investigate discrepancies
	Assist in preparing financial statements and management reports
	Support budgeting and forecasting processes
	Ensure accounting records comply with internal policies and regulatory standards
	Maintain proper documentation and filing of financial records
	Prepare schedules and documentation for internal and external audits


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting (BSc or equivalent)
	2 – 4 years of relevant accounting experience
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, or in view) is an advantage.
	Good understanding of accounting principles (IFRS/GAAP)
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting software (e.g., Sage, QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle)
	Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
	High level of accuracy, attention to detail, and organizational skills
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	Experience with reconciliations and financial reporting
	Ability to meet deadlines and work with minimal supervision
	Knowledge of tax filings and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
